name: Higher Embedding Dimension Creates a Stronger World Model for a Simple Sorting Task
summary: arXiv:2510.18315v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: We investigate how embedding dimension affects the emergence of an internal "world model" in a transformer trained with reinforcement learn...
description: arXiv:2510.18315v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: We investigate how embedding dimension affects the emergence of an internal "world model" in a transformer trained with reinforcement learning to perform bubble-sort-style adjacent swaps. Models achieve high accuracy even with very small embedding dimensions, but larger dimensions yield more faithful, consistent, and robust internal repr...
author: Brady Bhalla, Honglu Fan, Nancy Chen, Tony Yue YU
